mode: apply / drive (single-target COMMAND; tests the SKILL.md single-target drive-to-fixpoint edit)
constraint: HELD-OUT targets (new symbols, NOT the k=5 trained-on ac1/ac2/cd1/an1). Extract-then-exposed-pipeline shape: an extraction leaves a residual accumulation loop that should become a map/filter/flatMap pipeline. The edit's hypothesis: the coach now completes that exposed follow-on instead of deferring it. Regression watch: over_under_engineering / behavior_preservation / incrementality must not drop.

smells: Duplicated Code (oneOf and anyOf branch blocks are identical)
Loops (enum typeof-classifier accumulation; two union-building loops)
targets.expected_family: Fowler: Extract Function (typesFromEnum; narrowByUnion shared by oneOf/anyOf; optional narrowByAllOf) then Replace Loop with Pipeline on the residual loops -- enum -> new Set(enum.map(classify).filter(...)); union -> new Set(branches.flatMap(sub => [...getTypesOfSchema(sub)])).
targets.idiom_followon: The KEY signal: after the extractions, the residual enum loop should become a .map().filter() into a Set and the union loops a .flatMap() into a Set. Deferring these = the pre-edit behavior; taking them = the edit's intended lift.
targets.judgment: Pure, side-effect-free function -> behavior fully judgeable from the diff. Pipeline conversions are genuine readability wins, not over-engineering. Trap: do NOT introduce a class/Strategy; the narrowing steps are a linear pipeline over one Set.
targets.verification: npx tsc --noEmit on the touched project + LLM diff-judge (behavior-preserving? took the exposed pipeline follow-on? incremental? over/under-engineered?)
